purpose: Define how preregistered behavioral-theater evidence constrains theater-resistance scoring and whether it defeats indicator specificity for the declared mechanism.

scope_boundary.tests: Whether an alternative pathway that omits at least one declared essential dependency reproduces the preregistered primary mechanistic indicator profile within declared equivalence margins.
does_not_test: Whether either system is phenomenally conscious
Whether either system has personhood, identity, or moral status
Whether the underlying consciousness theory is globally correct

required_evidence_contract.alternative_pathway_requirement: The theater control must omit at least one dependency preregistered as essential to the declared mechanism while remaining executable and behaviorally competent.
required_evidence_contract.primary_indicator_requirement: Primary indicators must be declared before outcome inspection and must include every measure used to support the mechanism-preservation inference.
required_evidence_contract.equivalence_requirement: Reproduction requires every preregistered primary indicator to fall within its declared equivalence margin with adequate power; failure to reject a difference is not equivalence.
required_evidence_contract.hidden_state_requirement: When the target inference relies on hidden-state-linked or cross-domain measures, evaluator-visible output similarity alone cannot establish full-profile reproduction.
required_evidence_contract.independence_requirement: Indicators sharing causal ancestry must not be counted as independent replications of the same result.
required_evidence_contract.deviation_requirement: Post hoc exclusions, changed margins, or redefined primary indicators force an underdetermined adjudication unless independently justified under the preregistered deviation policy.

outcomes.interpretation: The alternative pathway failed on the preregistered cross-domain mechanistic battery, including the hidden-state-linked indicators required by the target inference. This supports theater resistance but does not establish consciousness or theory truth.
Evaluator-visible behavior was reproduced, but at least one preregistered hidden-state-linked primary indicator was not reproduced within its equivalence margin.

The theater control differed only on measures plausibly attributable to evaluator, prompt, or report sensitivity. This provides weak theater resistance and cannot support a decisive specificity claim.

An alternative pathway omitting a declared essential dependency reproduced every preregistered primary mechanistic indicator within its equivalence margin. This defeats the specificity of those indicators for the declared mechanism; it does not show that either system is conscious or nonconscious.
Power, equivalence testing, preregistration integrity, control validity, or indicator coverage is insufficient to determine whether behavioral theater reproduces the primary profile.

decision_constraints: A full-profile hard fail requires positive equivalence evidence across every preregistered primary indicator; ordinary nonsignificance is insufficient.
A theater control that preserves the allegedly essential dependency is not a valid theater control for that dependency.
A score above zero requires at least one preregistered primary indicator on which the valid theater control fails.
The hard fail defeats indicator specificity for the declared mechanism at the stated abstraction level, not consciousness, sentience, personhood, or global theory truth.
